page: Microsoft Azure E32bs_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EBSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage with optimized block storage performance.

Benchmark results place the E32bs_v5 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1899.88 and a multi-core score of 31080.56. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1662.5 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds reached 140.86 GB/sec and 65.94 GB/sec, respectively. In specialized tasks, the server demonstrates strong performance in Geekbench image processing and text generation for small 135M LLM models. However, large-scale LLM processing is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model dropping to a weak 6.67 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32bs_v5 offers high memory density and dedicated CPU resources without the added expense of local SSD storage. This makes it a viable option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but rely entirely on network-attached block storage. Appropriate workloads include database operations, memory-intensive web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale text and image processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32bs_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. This server does not include local storage or GPUs, focusing instead on remote Premium Storage block performance. Benchmarks show average CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1682.0 and a multi-core score of 14237.0. It exhibits strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation, though large-model LLM inference is weak. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require local disk storage, making it suitable for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32bs_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and optimized remote block storage performance for database and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32bs_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and remote block storage optimization.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors and optimized block storage performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Optimized for remote Premium Storage with no local storage overhead
